name: Tratto Netto
classification_notes: Name 'Tratto Netto' (Italian for 'clean line' or 'clean stroke') suggests a geometric or rationalist sans-serif. Likely drawn with even stroke widths and clean, unornamented terminals — consistent with Italian rationalist design tradition. Classification is inferred from the name and foundry context; direct specimen was not retrievable.
summary: A clean-lined sans-serif by Italian designer Joseph Miceli, sold directly through AlfaType Fonts Foundry with an accompanying alternates variant.
description: Tratto Netto — Italian for 'clean stroke' — is the signature typeface of AlfaType Fonts Foundry, a practice founded by Joseph Miceli in 2006. An alternates variant extends the design with additional glyph options. The foundry sells direct with modular, company-size-based licensing in euros.
foundry_blurb: AlfaType is a type-design practice established in 2006 by Joseph Miceli, developing an original catalogue of fonts and commissioned typefaces for print and screen.

name: Tratto Netto Alternates
classification_notes: Alternates variant of Tratto Netto. Shares the same underlying design but provides additional glyph alternates — likely stylistic or contextual substitutions. Classification mirrors the base family.
summary: The alternates companion to Tratto Netto, offering additional glyph variants for the base sans-serif design by Joseph Miceli.
description: Tratto Netto Alternates extends the base Tratto Netto family with a set of glyph alternatives, giving typographers additional options within the same rational, clean-lined framework. Like the base family, it is sold directly through AlfaType's website. Both families are priced at approximately €50 per licence tier.
foundry_blurb: AlfaType develops original typefaces and accepts commissions for custom type design for print and screen. Tratto Netto Alternates is the variant companion to the foundry's primary text face.
